edges.description: TBC1D24 loss not only disrupts synaptic vesicle cycling but also mislocalizes the v-ATPase V1 sector, so the same trafficking defect feeds into impaired endolysosomal acidification and synaptic vesicle reacidification.
edges.source: TBC1D24 loss of function impairs synaptic vesicle cycling

edges.description: Synaptic vesicle trafficking dysfunction disrupts neuronal signaling required for development and cognition.

edges.description: Severe synaptic vesicle dysfunction can produce early epileptic encephalopathy in TBC1D24-related disease.

nodes.description: Complete absence of nails can occur, representing the severe end of the onychodystrophy spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Deafness, onychodystrophy, osteodystrophy, mental retardation, and seizures (DOORS) syndrome: a new case report from Indonesia and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: absence of all finger- and toenails since birth

nodes.description: Beyond auditory neurons, TBC1D24 is expressed in glia-like non-sensory (supporting) epithelial cells of the developing cochlear sensory epithelium and is virtually absent from adjacent sensory hair cells, with the distinguishing expression disappearing around the onset of hearing. This suggests that disturbed vesicle trafficking in cochlear supporting cells, rather than a hair-cell-intrinsic defect alone, may contribute to the sensorineural deafness of DOORS syndrome.
nodes.id: Cochlear glia-like supporting cell vesicle trafficking dysfunction

evidence.reference_title: TBC1D24 is likely to regulate vesicle trafficking in glia-like non-sensory epithelial cells of the cochlea.
evidence.snippet:
TBC1D24 was detected in glia-like 
non-sensory epithelial cells during early developmental stages. In contrast, 
TBC1D24 was virtually absent in adjacent sensory hair cells.

nodes.description: A subset of TBC1D24-related DOORS individuals manifest a developmental and epileptic encephalopathy, including early infantile epileptic encephalopathy, which can be drug-resistant.

evidence.reference_title: The phenotypic landscape of a Tbc1d24 mutant mouse includes convulsive seizures resembling human early infantile epileptic encephalopathy.
evidence.snippet:
a recessive 
mutation of TBC1D24 associated with early infantile epileptic encephalopathy 
(EIEE)

nodes.description: Shortened or absent terminal (distal) phalanges of the hands and feet constitute the osteodystrophy component of DOORS.

evidence.reference_title: Deafness, onychodystrophy, osteodystrophy, mental retardation, and seizures (DOORS) syndrome: a new case report from Indonesia and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet:
X-rays of the hands and feet showed absence of the distal 
phalanx of her right and left fingers II-V and the distal phalanx of her right 
and left toes I-V

evidence.reference_title: Deafness, onychodystrophy, osteodystrophy, mental retardation, and seizures (DOORS) syndrome: a new case report from Indonesia and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet:
mainly characterized by sensorineural deafness, shortened terminal phalanges 
with small nails of hands and feet, intellectual deficit, and seizures

nodes.description: TBC1D24 interacts with the vacuolar ATPase (v-ATPase) in the brain and acts as a positive regulator of v-ATPase activity. Loss of TBC1D24 causes mislocalization of the V1 sector, increased pH in endo-lysosomal compartments, impaired autophagy, accumulation of lysosomes and non-degraded lipid material, and defective synaptic vesicle endocytosis and reacidification. The recurrent ATP6V1B2 p.Arg506* variant likewise impairs lysosomal acidification, defining a shared endolysosomal disease axis for DOORS syndrome.
nodes.id: Impaired v-ATPase activity and endolysosomal acidification

evidence.reference_title: TBC1D24 interacts with the v-ATPase and regulates intraorganellar pH in neurons.
evidence.snippet:
In Tbc1d24 knockout neurons, we detected V1 mis-localization with increased pH 
at endo-lysosomal compartments and autophagy impairment. Furthermore, synaptic 
vesicles endocytosis and reacidification were impaired.

evidence.reference_title: TBC1D24 interacts with the v-ATPase and regulates intraorganellar pH in neurons.
evidence.snippet:
we demonstrate 
that TBC1D24 is a positive regulator of v-ATPase activity in neurons

evidence.reference_title: Clinicopathological Relationships in an Aged Case of DOORS Syndrome With a p.Arg506X Mutation in the ATP6V1B2 Gene.
evidence.snippet:
DOORS [deafness, onychodystrophy, osteodystrophy, intellectual disability 
(mental retardation), and seizures] syndrome can be caused by mutations in the 
TBC1D24 and ATP6V1B2 genes, both of which are involved in endolysosomal 
function.

nodes.description: Intellectual disability or developmental delay (historically the "R" for mental retardation) is a core feature, frequently severe.

evidence.reference_title: TBC1D24-Related Disorders.
evidence.snippet: intellectual disability / developmental delay, and seizures

evidence.reference_title: DOORS syndrome and a recurrent truncating ATP6V1B2 variant.
evidence.snippet:
All families but one had developmental delay or intellectual disability and five 
individuals had epilepsy.

nodes.description: Microcephaly is reported in approximately one third of affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: The genetic basis of DOORS syndrome: an exome-sequencing study.
evidence.snippet:
Deafness, onychodystrophy, osteodystrophy, mental retardation, and 
seizures (DOORS) syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder of unknown 
cause.

nodes.description: Dystrophic, small, or absent nails of the fingers and toes (onychodystrophy / anonychia), one of the defining DOORS features.

evidence.snippet: Deafness was present in all individuals, along with onychodystrophy and abnormal fingers and/or toes.

nodes.description: Visual impairment with optic atrophy/optic neuropathy is reported among the variable additional manifestations of DOORS syndrome; ophthalmologic involvement such as strabismus is also documented.

nodes.description: Peripheral neuropathy is among the additional reported neurologic manifestations of DOORS syndrome.

standard treatment for tremors, dystonic

evidence.reference_title: DOORS syndrome and a recurrent truncating ATP6V1B2 variant.
evidence.snippet:
We identified the same truncating variant in ATP6V1B2 
(NM_001693.4:c.1516C>T; p.Arg506*) in nine individuals from eight unrelated 
families with DOORS syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Clinicopathological Relationships in an Aged Case of DOORS Syndrome With a p.Arg506X Mutation in the ATP6V1B2 Gene.
evidence.snippet:
DOORS [deafness, onychodystrophy, osteodystrophy, intellectual disability 
(mental retardation), and seizures] syndrome can be caused by mutations in the 
TBC1D24 and ATP6V1B2 genes, both of which are involved in endolysosomal 
function.

nodes.description: Seizures occur in most affected individuals and usually start in the first year of life. Seizure types include generalized tonic-clonic, complex partial, focal clonic, and infantile spasms; some cases are drug-resistant.

evidence.reference_title: The genetic basis of DOORS syndrome: an exome-sequencing study.
evidence.snippet:
The 
seizure types in individuals with TBC1D24 mutations included generalised 
tonic-clonic, complex partial, focal clonic, and infantile spasms.

nodes.description: Profound, typically prelingual sensorineural deafness is a cardinal feature, present in essentially all affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: TBC1D24-Related Disorders.
evidence.snippet:
DOORS syndrome (deafness, onychodystrophy, osteodystrophy, mental retardation, 
and seizures), with profound sensorineural hearing loss, onychodystrophy,

evidence.reference_title: DOORS syndrome and a recurrent truncating ATP6V1B2 variant.
evidence.snippet: Deafness was present in all individuals, along with onychodystrophy and abnormal fingers and/or toes.

nodes.description: TBC1D24 is a vesicle-associated protein linked to Rab/ARF6 small-GTPase regulation and synaptic vesicle trafficking. Biallelic loss-of-function impairs presynaptic endocytosis and synaptic vesicle recycling, disrupting neurotransmission. In hippocampal neurons TBC1D24 localizes to clathrin-coated vesicles and synapses, consistent with a presynaptic trafficking role underlying epilepsy and neurodevelopmental impairment.
nodes.id: TBC1D24 loss of function impairs synaptic vesicle cycling

evidence.reference_title: DOORS syndrome and a recurrent truncating ATP6V1B2 variant.
evidence.snippet:
Biallelic variants in TBC1D24, which encodes a protein that regulates 
vesicular transport, are frequently identified in patients with DOORS

evidence.reference_title: The phenotypic landscape of a Tbc1d24 mutant mouse includes convulsive seizures resembling human early infantile epileptic encephalopathy.
evidence.snippet:
TBC1D24 is associated with 
clathrin-coated vesicles and synapses of hippocampal neurons, suggesting a 
crucial role of TBC1D24 in vesicle trafficking important for neuronal signal 
transmission.

nodes.description: Triphalangeal thumb is reported in roughly one third of affected individuals and was documented radiographically in a genetically confirmed DOORS case.

evidence.reference_title: Clinicopathological Relationships in an Aged Case of DOORS Syndrome With a p.Arg506X Mutation in the ATP6V1B2 Gene.
evidence.snippet:
This 
Caucasian male patient, who died at the age of 72 years, presented all the 
typical cardinal signs of DOORS syndrome.
